hypothesis: This is related to fried chicken with potatoes.

nodes.name: Fried Chicken with Potatoes
nodes.description: name: Fried Chicken with Potatoes | short_description: Crispy, golden fried chicken served with roasted or pan-fried potatoes — a comforting, savory dinner swap that replaces the roast variation with
nodes.premise: Meal: Fried Chicken with Potatoes - name: Fried Chicken with Potatoes | short_description: Crispy, golden fried chicken served with roasted or pan-fried potatoes — a comforting, savory dinner swap that replaces the roast variation with a classic fried preparation. | meal_suggestion: Season bone-in chicken pieces (or your preferred cuts) with salt, pepper, and a blend of paprika, garlic powder, and a touch of cayenne. Dredge in seasoned flour, dip in beaten egg, then coat with panko or extra flour. Fry in hot oil (175–180°C / 350–360°F) until cooked through and crisp, or shallow-fry in a skillet for a lighter finish. For the potatoes, cut baby potatoes or Yukon golds into halves or wedges, toss with olive oil, salt, pepper, and rosemary, then roast at 200°C (400°F) until golden and tender, or pan-fry until crisp. Serve the fried chicken alongside the potatoes with a simple green salad or steamed vegetables and lemon wedges. | ingredients: {'name': 'chicken pieces (bone-in or preferred cuts)'}, {'name': 'all-purpose flour'}, {'name': 'panko breadcrumbs (optional for extra crispiness)'}, {'name': 'eggs'}, {'name': 'paprika'}, {'name': 'garlic powder'}, {'name': 'cayenne pepper (optional)'}, {'name': 'salt'}, {'name': 'black pepper'}, {'name': 'vegetable oil or frying oil'}, {'name': 'baby potatoes or Yukon gold potatoes'}, {'name': 'olive oil'}, {'name': 'rosemary or dried herbs'}, {'name': 'lemon (for serving)'}, {'name': 'mixed greens or steamed vegetables (optional side)'} | nutrition_note: Fried chicken is higher in fat and calories compared with roasted preparations due to added frying oil and breading. Pairing with potatoes provides carbohydrate energy and, when potatoes are roasted or pan-fried with minimal oil, preserves fiber and potassium. To reduce overall fat, use an air-fryer or shallow-pan method, remove excess oil with paper towels, and serve with a generous portion of vegetables or salad to balance the meal.
